verses.text: As the armies gather, the Philistine lords spot David among Achish's troops and demand, 'What do these Hebrews here?' His compromise has placed him where he never should have been — about to march against his own people. Sin's path leads to corners with no good exit.

As the armies gather, the Philistine lords spot David among Achish's troops and demand, 'What do these Hebrews here?' His compromise has placed him where he never should have been — about to march against his own people. Sin's path leads to corners with no good exit.

As the armies gather, the Philistine lords spot David among Achish's troops and demand, 'What do these Hebrews here?' His compromise has placed him where he never should have been — about to march against his own people. Sin's path leads to corners with no good exit.

The lords insist David be sent away, fearing he will turn on them in battle, and they quote the old song of his thousands slain. God uses even the suspicion of enemies to rescue David from an impossible position. He is delivered not by his own wit but by providence.

The lords insist David be sent away, fearing he will turn on them in battle, and they quote the old song of his thousands slain. God uses even the suspicion of enemies to rescue David from an impossible position. He is delivered not by his own wit but by providence.

Achish, who trusts David, reluctantly tells him to go home in peace. The very favour David won by deceit now sends him safely out of the trap. God can untangle the snares we walk into, sparing us the worst fruit of our folly when we cannot save ourselves.

Achish, who trusts David, reluctantly tells him to go home in peace. The very favour David won by deceit now sends him safely out of the trap. God can untangle the snares we walk into, sparing us the worst fruit of our folly when we cannot save ourselves.

David protests his dismissal — whether sincerely or to keep up the act we cannot tell — but Achish is firm, and David rises early to return. So God shuts the door David should never have opened. Sometimes the kindest providence is the one that simply will not let us proceed.
